Ethiopia (modern borders) — khat origin + largest producer
description: Modern political border (post-1991). Khat's ORIGIN + world's largest producer. Harar + Oromia highlands. #2 foreign-exchange earner after coffee. Legal + culturally central.

Yemen (modern borders) — daily chewing session at national scale
description: Modern political border. Estimated 70% of Yemen's water use goes to khat cultivation — climate-crisis multiplier. Tafrita (afternoon chewing) is a central institution. Civil war since 2015 has disrupted but not stopped the trade.

Somalia + Djibouti (modern borders) — major consumer markets
description: Modern political borders. Somalia may be the world's largest per-capita khat market. Djibouti serves as transit + consumption corridor. Imports flown in daily from Kenya + Ethiopia.

Kenya (modern borders) — Meru miraa trade
description: Modern political border (post-1963). Meru County 'miraa' is air-freighted daily to Somalia + UK diaspora pre-ban. Regional economic anchor; miraa trade dominated by women entrepreneurs.

UK (modern borders) — banned 2014
description: Modern political border. UK banned khat (Class C drug) in 2014, despite Home Office advisory council recommending no ban. Disrupted Somali + Yemeni diaspora economies in Cardiff, Birmingham, London.
